lesson: Direct A-trace local search is insufficient once hidden-state risk, tail risk, and redundancy are optimized jointly.
The regime benefits from more aggressive searched one-exchange refinement because the declared budget often binds before local stability.
Large networks need both calibrated risk estimation and more search budget to convert a weak margin into a stable paired win.
Thin validation windows can mis-rank low-budget layouts; calibrating reconstruction risk on train+validation resolves the row.
The dominant gain is robust direct optimization of reconstruction risk over the prior TRACE-SL swap family under the same evaluation protocol.
